title: Carphephorus paniculatus
intro: Carphephorus paniculatus, also known as Rosinweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Carphephorus paniculatus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Rosinweed is made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in salves and ointments for topical application, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
taxonomy: Carphephorus paniculatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is often classified as a member of the tribe Heliantheae. Carphephorus paniculatus is a perennial herb that is commonly known as flat-topped white aster. It is a member of the genus Carphephorus within the family Asteraceae.
uses: Carphephorus paniculatus is a plant species that has been found to have potential medicinal uses. However, research on its specific properties and applications is limited. Further studies are needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Carphephorus paniculatus for medicinal purposes.
